Title: Checkout - Select Saved Address

Title: Checkout Validation - XSS Prevention in Address Fields
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User at checkout shipping address step, cart has at least one item
Steps: [NAV] Navigate to checkout shipping address step [ACT] Enter First Name: '' [ACT] Enter Last Name: 'TestXSS' [ACT] Enter Phone: '' [ACT] Fill Street, City, State, ZIP with valid data [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify no XSS alert dialog is triggered (no JavaScript execution) [ACT] Click Continue and wait for form to submit or advance [ASSERT] If checkout advances: navigate to confirmation; verify First Name shown does not execute script (literal text, not rendered HTML) [ASSERT] Verify no alert dialog appeared during or after submission [ACT] Enter Phone: '5551234' [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify checkout does not execute embedded script [ACT] Fix all fields: First Name 'John', Phone '555-123-4567' [ACT] Click Continue [ASSERT] Verify form advances with clean data
Assertions:
[DOM] No alert dialog triggered at any point (critical security assertion)
[STATE] No client-side script execution from injected HTML payload
[STATE] Server strips or escapes HTML tags — output shown as literal text (e.g., '<script>' or stripped entirely), NOT rendered/executed HTML
[STATE] Clean data accepted and checkout proceeds after correcting fields
NOTE: No inline client-side validation error is expected for HTML tags — form may accept input and rely on server-side sanitization; this is acceptable behavior. Inline error NOT required.
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No XSS alert dialogs; no uncaught JS errors from injected payload
[API] addOrUpdateCartShipment mutation data does not contain unsanitized executable script
[NETWORK] No 5xx errors on shipment update
Failure_Signals: Alert dialog fires from injected script; HTML renders and executes in name field on confirmation; server returns 500 from unsanitized input
References: VCST-4803 coverage gap — checkout address XSS
Automation_Status: Generated — Rewritten 2026-04-21: removed incorrect assertion [DOM] Error messages shown inline for fields containing HTML tags. Observed behavior: no inline error; server strips/escapes HTML. Assertions updated to structural security invariants (no script execution, server sanitization).

Title: Minimum Order Amount - Checkout Blocked When Below Minimum
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
ADMIN-CONFIG REQUIRED: Store must have minimum order amount configured in Admin > Stores > General > MinOrderTotal (e.g., $50.00). QA environment does not have this configured by default. Admin step: navigate to Stores > [Store Name] > edit > set MinOrderTotal = 50.00 and save. Then verify {{TEST_SKU}} price is below $50 (e.g., single item < $5).
Steps:
[NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and add a low-value item to cart (total below minimum order amount)
[NAV] navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] verify cart total is below configured minimum order amount
[ACT] attempt to click 'Proceed to Checkout' or 'Place Order'
[WAIT] wait for validation response
[ASSERT] verify checkout blocked with informational message
[ASSERT] verify message indicates minimum order amount and shortfall (e.g., 'Minimum order is $50.00. Add $45.00 more to proceed.')
Assertions:
[DOM] 'Proceed to Checkout' button disabled or validation message shown
[DOM] message references minimum order amount value
[DOM] message references shortfall amount (amount needed to reach minimum)
[STATE] user cannot advance past cart or checkout steps while below minimum
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] getCart or checkout mutation returns minimum order violation error
[CONSOLE] no JS errors when minimum order block is triggered
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx (validation should be graceful)
Failure_Signals: checkout proceeds despite cart being below minimum; no message shown; vague error message without minimum amount or shortfall; 500 error instead of validation message
Cleanup: Remove test items from cart; reset MinOrderTotal to 0 in Admin
Automation_Status: Manual — BLOCKED: requires admin config (MinOrderTotal) not set in QA. SEED verdict 2026-04-21.

Title: Saved Credit Card - Checkout Completes Without Re-entering Details
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]

Title: Session Timeout During Checkout - Cart Preserved After Re-authentication

TOOL-LIMITATION: Natural session timeout in QA environment is too long for automated testing. Use cookie-clearing approach: MCP browser_evaluate to delete access_token and refresh_token cookies, then proceed. This simulates session expiry without waiting for timeout. [NAV] log in to {{FRONT_URL}} and add items to cart; proceed to checkout payment step
[ASSERT] note cart contents (item names, quantities, total)
[ACT] Clear auth cookies via browser DevTools (manually delete access_token/refresh_token cookies) to simulate session expiry
[ACT] attempt to interact with checkout (e.g., click 'Place Order')
[WAIT] wait for response
[ASSERT] verify user redirected to login page or session expired message shown (not a 500 error)
[ACT] log in again with {{USER_EMAIL}} and {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[WAIT] wait for redirect after login
[ASSERT] verify cart contents preserved (same items and quantities as before session expired)
[ASSERT] verify user can resume checkout flow
Assertions:
[DOM] session expiry triggers login redirect or graceful message (not 500)
[STATE] after re-login, cart contains same items and quantities
[STATE] user can proceed through checkout after re-authentication
[DOM] no 'internal server error' or blank page on session expiry
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] requests with expired token return 401 (not 500)
[CONSOLE] no unhandled exceptions on session timeout; 401 handled gracefully
[NETWORK] 401 response on expired session; 200 on successful re-auth
Failure_Signals: 500 error on session expiry; cart emptied after re-login; user redirected to homepage with no message; checkout state lost entirely
Cleanup: Clear test cart items; re-login if needed
Automation_Status: Manual — Rewritten 2026-04-21: natural timeout too long; use cookie-clearing to simulate. TOOL-LIMITATION verdict.

Title: Journey: Guest-to-Auth Transition With Address Popup Availability
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
Browser in clean anonymous session (no auth cookie — use private/incognito mode or clear cookies). {{USER_EMAIL}} is an org-affiliated user with at least 3 saved addresses. {{TEST_SKU}} is available in stock and can be added to cart as a guest.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] storefront home page loaded
[ASSERT] Sign In link or button visible in header — confirms anonymous/guest session
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/product/{{TEST_SKU}}
[WAIT] product detail page loaded
[ACT] click 'Add to Cart' button
[WAIT] success notification visible OR cart badge increments
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded with {{TEST_SKU}} visible
[ASSERT] Shipping Details section shows an inline one-off address entry form (NOT a 'Please select a shipping address' popup CTA)
[ASSERT] no popup trigger present in Shipping Details for anonymous user
[ACT] click 'Sign In' link or button in header
[WAIT] sign-in page or modal visible
[ACT] fill 'Email': {{USER_EMAIL}}
[ACT] fill 'Password': {{USER_PASSWORD}}
[ACT] click 'Sign In' or 'Login' button
[WAIT] authenticated — user name visible in header
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded
[ASSERT] cart contains item {{TEST_SKU}} (guest-to-auth cart merge per BL-CART-008)
[ASSERT] Shipping Details section now shows 'Please select a shipping address' link or pencil icon (popup-enabled flow replaces inline form after auth)
[ACT] click 'Please select a shipping address' link or pencil icon
[WAIT] address selection popup opens
[ASSERT] popup lists at least 3 saved address rows (the authenticated user's addresses)
[ACT] click any address row
[WAIT] popup closes; Shipping Details updates to selected address
[ACT] click user name in top-right header to open account-menu popup
[ACT] click 'Logout' in account-menu popup (selector data-testid="main-layout.top-header.account-menu.sign-out-button")
[WAIT] signed out — Sign In link visible in header
[ASSERT] cart clears or shows guest empty state (authenticated cart not preserved in anonymous session per BL-CART-008 / BL-AUTH-001)
Assertions:
[DOM] as anonymous: Shipping Details contains inline address form fields (not popup CTA)
[DOM] as anonymous: no 'Please select a shipping address' popup trigger present
[STATE] after sign-in: cart contains item {{TEST_SKU}} — guest cart merged into authenticated cart (BL-CART-008)
[DOM] after sign-in: Shipping Details shows 'Please select a shipping address' link or pencil icon
[DOM] popup opens and lists at least 3 saved addresses with keyword search input and facet dropdowns
[DOM] Shipping Details reflects the address selected from the popup
[STATE] after sign-out: cart clears or shows guest empty state (no authenticated items persisted)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] as anonymous: no currentCustomerAddresses network request fired to /graphql (verify in network tab — absence confirms BL-CHK-001 guest isolation)
[API] after sign-in: currentCustomerAddresses called on popup open; errors[] is empty; items count >= 3
[API] cart query lineItems contain {{TEST_SKU}} before and after sign-in (confirms BL-CART-008 merge preserved item)
[API] addOrUpdateCartShipment (or address selection mutation) returns HTTP 200; errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during anonymous add-to-cart, sign-in transition, popup interaction, or sign-out
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on any /graphql request; no 401 errors in authenticated cart context after sign-in
Failure_Signals: inline address form absent for anonymous user (popup CTA shown to guest — BL-CHK-001 violation); cart loses {{TEST_SKU}} after sign-in (BL-CART-008 merge failure — cart badge shows 0); popup CTA absent after sign-in despite saved addresses (feature toggle off or composable regression); popup opens blank or spinner >5s; currentCustomerAddresses fired while still anonymous (data scope leak); 4xx/5xx on any post-auth /graphql call
Cleanup: Sign out if not already signed out; remove any remaining test items from cart; no addresses were created so no address cleanup needed
References: Promoted from VCST-4710-SF-022 / real-user-behavior feedback 2026-04-23; VCST-4710 / PR #129; BL-AUTH-001; BL-CART-008; BL-B2B-001; BL-CHK-001
